titleEn: 10th International Forum on the Evaluation of the Quality of Research and Higher Education (FECIES)
excerptEn: The technical heads of Teaching Staff Training at the four public universities of Castilla y León contribute, through their participation in a symposium entitled “Evaluation and Quality of university teaching staff training”, the perspective of “collaboration between institutions”, with the aim of supporting and making more efficient the…
contentEn:
The technical heads of Teaching Staff Training at the four public universities of Castilla y León contribute, through their participation in a symposium entitled “Evaluation and Quality of university teaching staff training”, the perspective of “collaboration between institutions”, with the aim of supporting and making more efficient the teacher training programmes in their respective institutions. The presentation will take place on 25 June, from 12:30 to 14:00, at the University of Granada.
Further information about the 10th Forum at http://www.ugr.es/~aepc/XFORO/presentacion.html.

titleEn: Inter-university course Virtual Training 2.0
excerptEn: Monday 20 May saw the start of the first inter-university online teacher training experience within the University of Salamanca's 2013 Teaching Staff Training Plan. It is an initiative under the Collaboration Agreement on Teacher Training and Innovation between the four public universities of Castilla y León. The…
contentEn:
Monday 20 May saw the start of the first inter-university online teacher training experience within the University of Salamanca's 2013 Teaching Staff Training Plan. It is an initiative under the Collaboration Agreement on Teacher Training and Innovation between the four public universities of Castilla y León.

The course, Virtual Training 2.0, brings together teaching staff from the University of Burgos, the University of León, the University of Salamanca and the University of Valladolid. A total of 50 lecturers will work on the educational use of social networks and 2.0 tools. The course is run entirely online, tutored by a team of expert teachers with individualised monitoring of those enrolled.

titleEn: Interview with Prof. Dr. Juan A. Juanes Méndez, Deputy Director of the IUCE
excerptEn: We interview Prof. Dr. Juan A. Juanes Méndez, Deputy Director of the Institute for Research in Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, IUCE. A university senior lecturer who teaches on numerous master's programmes, he holds a doctorate in Medicine and Surgery from the University of Salamanca. He also leads the Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems Research Group recognised by…
contentEn:
We interview Prof. Dr. Juan A. Juanes Méndez, Deputy Director of the Institute for Research in Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, IUCE. A university senior lecturer who teaches on numerous master's programmes, he holds a doctorate in Medicine and Surgery from the University of Salamanca. He also leads the Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems Research Group recognised by the USAL (VisualMed System). He is a member of the expert panel of the National Agency for Quality Assessment and Accreditation of the higher education system (ANECA).

He is co-author of 19 books of a teaching nature. He has collaborated on various projects with different hospitals and university centres across the country. He has more than 120 articles in national and international journals and has received 20 research and teaching awards, from Royal Academies of Medicine and for papers presented at congresses and medical film festivals.

– What are the academic challenges facing the University Institute of Education Sciences in training and research?

The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca (IUCE) is an interdisciplinary institute, specialising in Research and Higher Education, created to foster and put into practice scientific initiatives in education, technological development and transfer to the educational sphere.

Among our most recent academic challenges, we have developed the new Doctoral Programme in Education and the Knowledge Society, whose fundamental objective is the acquisition of the competences and skills related to high-quality scientific research in interdisciplinary study in the sciences. This programme, established within the European Higher Education Area (EHEA), takes into account the new foundations of the Revised

Lisbon Agenda, as well as the construction of the European Research Area (ERA).

– What new-generation technological tools does the IUCE use in its academic training model?

The Institute's research lines are supported mainly by six Recognised Research Groups of the University of Salamanca associated with the IUCE (GRIAL, GITE, OCA, VISUALMED, Robotics and Society and, lastly, E-LECTRA). These groups work on the following research lines:

  1. Educational assessment and guidance.
  2. Interaction and eLearning.
  3. Research and innovation in educational technology.
  4. Media and education.
  5. Medicine and education.
  6. Educational robotics.
  7. Engineering and education.
  8. Education and the information society.

The research groups use an extensive battery of technological tools to develop virtual training systems: from commercial software of various kinds to technologies such as, for example, chroma key.

The robotics and engineering groups, in coordination with the Advanced Medical Visualisation group (VisualMed System), work on simulation systems.

Among state-of-the-art tools, we would also highlight the use of learning technologies (LMS/LCMS platforms, eLearning ecosystems, learning objects, eLearning standards and specifications, authoring tools, educational software, adaptive educational hypermedia systems, intelligent tutors); and Web engineering and software architecture (software processes for the Web, the Semantic Web, ontologies, software quality, knowledge management, design processes, service-based architectures, model-driven development, MDA, Web services).

– How has teaching evolved in the medical-scientific context with the application of new technologies?

The development of computer applications in training environments is being pushed forward very strongly, with three-dimensional visualisation programs being produced. In this way, users are provided with an excellent environment for visualising teaching content, which delivers a high degree of assimilation of concepts.

New technologies based on three-dimensional imaging are becoming highly relevant for understanding, approaching and studying different structures, which undoubtedly results in improved training for the users who work with them.

The close collaboration the IUCE maintains with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology (CITEC-B) has facilitated the implementation and development of these tools for teaching in the medical-scientific environment. CITEC-B and the IUCE research and work on the development of different three-dimensional visualisation tools that help health professionals with training and the assimilation of knowledge.

– How do you expect the application of new technologies in medical training to evolve in the coming years?

In the medical-scientific context, new technologies applied to training are moving mainly towards the use of medical image simulation environments, which makes for an excellent learning curve thanks to their effectiveness and speed. Simulation will make it possible to resolve the difficulties arising, for example, from a clinical examination, while also providing greater safety for real patients.

There is no doubt that the advances in computing seen in recent years, the growing awareness of the need to produce digital content of excellent quality, and the improvement of communication infrastructures have been fundamental in enabling these advanced technological tools to spread, in the field of training, to all levels of the different areas of knowledge, including Medicine. Today we find very powerful applications that faithfully reflect reality.

titleEn: An IUCE working group receives a Corselas Education Award
excerptEn: The “Conectados” research team, belonging to the University Institute of Education Sciences, has received the “Fundación Vicente y García Corselas” award. The award was presented at the Saint Thomas Aquinas ceremony held by the University of Salamanca on 28 January in the Paraninfo.
contentEn: The “Conectados” research team, belonging to the University Institute of Education Sciences, has received the “Fundación Vicente y García Corselas” award. The award was presented at the Saint Thomas Aquinas ceremony held by the University of Salamanca on 28 January in the Paraninfo.

titleEn: Biennial Seminar: Good practice workshop of the UNESCO Chair in University Management and Policy
excerptEn: The fourth session, entitled “Good Practice Workshop: presentation of experiences”, was held with the aim of sharing successful experiences carried out at different universities, which could serve as a reference for the rest and contribute to the overall improvement of the university system, as well as recognising the achievements these universities have made in recent…
contentEn: The fourth session, entitled “Good Practice Workshop: presentation of experiences”, was held with the aim of sharing successful experiences carried out at different universities, which could serve as a reference for the rest and contribute to the overall improvement of the university system, as well as recognising the achievements these universities have made in recent years. To this end, a public call was organised, from which the most relevant cases were selected to be presented during the session.

titleEn: Report on the Peer Tutoring project
The IUCE and CITEC-B receive awards at VIDEOMED 2012
excerptEn: The Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca, together with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology of Madrid (CITEC-B), with which the Institute holds a collaboration agreement, have been honoured with four awards at the XVII International Festival of Health and Medical films and Telemedicine…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca, together with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology of Madrid (CITEC-B), with which the Institute holds a collaboration agreement, have been honoured with four awards at the XVII International Festival of Health and Medical films and Telemedicine (VIDEOMED 2012), held in Badajoz from 13 to 16 November 2012.

The works were presented by Professor Dr Juan A. Juanes Méndez, of the University of Salamanca and Deputy Director of the IUCE. The audiovisual materials submitted to the competition are the fruit of work carried out between different university centres in our country, together with the collaboration of prestigious clinicians from various hospitals across Spain.

The titles presented and honoured at Videomed 2012 were the following:

  • Advances in medical visualisation for the management of patients with multiple sclerosis.
  • Advanced medical visualisation environment for psoriatic pathology.
  • Respiratory diseases: a virtual teaching-learning environment.
  • Simulation ultrasound viewer for the virtual examination of the female pelvis.

titleEn: Antonio Vaquero Award in Educational Informatics
excerptEn: The GRIAL research group, attached to the IUCE, has been honoured with the “Antonio Vaquero Award in Educational Informatics” for the best article at the SIIE 2012 International Conference (International Symposium on Computers in Education), held in Andorra from 29 to 31 October 2012, for the paper entitled “Perception of the openness of LMSs in the…
contentEn:
The GRIAL research group, attached to the IUCE, has been honoured with the “Antonio Vaquero Award in Educational Informatics” for the best article at the SIIE 2012 International Conference (International Symposium on Computers in Education), held in Andorra from 29 to 31 October 2012, for the paper entitled “Perception of the openness of LMSs in the educational and technological branches”.

The award, established by the Association for the Development of Educational Informatics, aims to reward the best paper presented at the SIIE. In deciding the winner, account was taken not only of the assessment reports by the reviewers of the accepted articles, but also of the report by the chair of the session in which it was presented.

The award was presented by Antonio Vaquero, who said a few words at the ceremony at which the accrediting diploma was presented to Miguel A. Conde, one of the authors of the award-winning article: “The importance of this award will come not from its name, but from the quality of the award-winning works and the prestige of their authors. We could not have made a better start. The winning paper, ‘Perception of the openness of LMSs in the educational and technological branches’, by Miguel A. Conde, Francisco J. García-Peñalvo, María J. Rodríguez-Conde and Marc Alier, is of the highest quality, and its authors are regarded as members of one of the most prestigious university groups in Educational Informatics. This unbeatable start augurs a brilliant future.”

titleEn: The E-LECTRA group receives the 1st Research Award in Publishing
excerptEn: The University of Salamanca research group E-LECTRA: Electronic Publishing and Reading, Transfer and Automated Information Retrieval has been honoured with the 1st National Research Award on Publishing and the Knowledge Society, organised by the Union of Spanish University Publishers (UNE), in recognition of its work entitled “The Ecosystem of the…
contentEn:
The University of Salamanca research group E-LECTRA: Electronic Publishing and Reading, Transfer and Automated Information Retrieval has been honoured with the 1st National Research Award on Publishing and the Knowledge Society, organised by the Union of Spanish University Publishers (UNE), in recognition of its work entitled “The Ecosystem of the University Electronic Book”.

The jury of the competition, decided during the “Liber” book fair held in Barcelona, considers that the work will become an essential reference in research studies on publishing, and highlighted the interesting analysis it provides of the current situation of university publishing.

The research was carried out by the E-LECTRA group members José Antonio Cordón García (director of the research group), Raquel Gómez Díaz, Julio Alonso Arévalo and José Luis Alonso Berrocal, several of whom are active members of the IUCE.

Further information.

titleEn: The 2012 PDI Training Plan draws to a close
excerptEn: Wednesday 12 September brought to a close this year's edition of the 2012 University Teaching Staff Training Plan. Since January, the IUCE has run a total of 72 activities in which 638 hours of training have been delivered and a total of 1414 places have been allocated, covering more than 50%…
contentEn:
Wednesday 12 September brought to a close this year's edition of the 2012 University Teaching Staff Training Plan.

Since January, the IUCE has run a total of 72 activities in which 638 hours of training have been delivered and a total of 1414 places have been allocated, covering more than 50% of the demand received.

Worth highlighting is the aim of familiarising teaching staff with the teaching methodologies and the student assessment process entailed by the European Higher Education Area, with particular emphasis on the use of the Studium virtual platform and on the use of IT tools and digital audiovisual technology in teaching practice.

An important part of this programme is the 2012 Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff, which aims to equip new lecturers with the competences they need to begin their careers as university teaching professionals.

Pictured are some of the participants who successfully completed this edition of the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU), together with Mª José Rodríguez Conde and Susana Olmos Miguelánez, coordinators of the programme.

titleEn: Launch of the book Multiculturalism in Technology-Based Education
excerptEn: The American publisher IGI Global published in September 2012 the edited book 'Multiculturalism in Technology-Based Education: Case Studies on ICT-Supported Approaches', which aims to explore multidisciplinary approaches to transculturality and multiculturality and their influence on technology-based education. To this end, Francisco J. García Peñalvo…
contentEn:
The American publisher IGI Global published in September 2012 the edited book ‘Multiculturalism in Technology-Based Education: Case Studies on ICT-Supported Approaches’, which aims to explore multidisciplinary approaches to transculturality and multiculturality and their influence on technology-based education. To this end, Francisco J. García Peñalvo, editor of the publication and director of the GRIAL research group attached to this Institute, has selected a series of case studies that investigate transcultural education through theoretical aspects and practical applications within a technological framework.

Several members of the IUCE, as well as experts from other Spanish and international universities, have contributed their experience and expertise on the subject.

Both the book and the individual chapters are available in print or e-book format through the publisher's website.

titleEn: The Rasch measurement model
excerptEn: On 9 and 10 July the course “The Rasch measurement model” was held in the IUCE classrooms, taught by Dr Manuel Jorge González-Montesinos Martínez, who holds an MRM doctorate (University of Arizona, 2004) and is Tenured Professor-Researcher “C” in the Department of Social Sciences at the University of Sonora (Mexico). Organised by the…
contentEn:
On 9 and 10 July the course “The Rasch measurement model” was held in the IUCE classrooms, taught by Dr Manuel Jorge González-Montesinos Martínez, who holds an MRM doctorate (University of Arizona, 2004) and is Tenured Professor-Researcher “C” in the Department of Social Sciences at the University of Sonora (Mexico).

Organised by the Educational Assessment and Guidance Group of the University Institute of Education Sciences and by the area of Research Methods and Diagnosis in Education of the University of Salamanca, the workshop comprised 2 practical sessions of 4 hours each, with the aim of providing a training space for the practice and interpretation of Rasch modelling in scales with achievement and perception items.

titleEn: Master's in eLearning, among the best postgraduate programmes in Spain
excerptEn: On 20 June 2012 the newspaper EL MUNDO published the ninth edition of its 250 MASTER special, a guide featuring a selection of the 250 best master's degrees offered in Spain, designed to help university graduates choose a specialisation. The guide groups these programmes into 15…
contentEn:
On 20 June 2012 the newspaper EL MUNDO published the ninth edition of its 250 MASTER special, a guide featuring a selection of the 250 best master's degrees offered in Spain, designed to help university graduates choose a specialisation.

The guide groups these programmes into 15 categories, corresponding to a total of 50 areas of knowledge, in each of which the five best master's degrees are reviewed. The 250 postgraduate degrees were selected after an exhaustive analysis of more than 1,000 postgraduate programmes and more than 500 opinions from teaching staff, on the basis of 26 quality criteria including human resources, material resources, the syllabus and the methodology.

The Master's in eLearning: Technologies and methods for online training directed by Francisco J. García Peñalvo, a member of the IUCE and director of the GRIAL group, is among the five best in the Teaching area, alongside degrees from the Complutense University of Madrid and the Autonomous University of Barcelona. It is also the only eLearning master's degree at a Spanish university to appear in the ranking published by the newspaper El Mundo.

titleEn: Meeting of ICE Directors
excerptEn: On 1 June 2012 the annual meeting of Directors of ICEs and university teacher training services took place in Córdoba, with the aim of beginning work on the creation of the University Teaching Staff Training Network.
contentEn: On 1 June 2012 the annual meeting of Directors of ICEs and university teacher training services took place in Córdoba, with the aim of beginning work on the creation of the University Teaching Staff Training Network.

titleEn: Monitoring of the Bologna Process
excerptEn: Education Ministers from the 47 countries taking part in the Bologna Process met in Bucharest on 26 April 2012 to review progress on higher education reforms and set priorities for the next three years. The report on the implementation of the Bologna Process in Europe…
contentEn:
Education Ministers from the 47 countries taking part in the Bologna Process met in Bucharest on 26 April 2012 to review progress on higher education reforms and set priorities for the next three years.

The report on the implementation of the Bologna Process in Europe can be consulted on the website of the European Commission.

titleEn: The TALARIA project at the University of Salamanca
excerptEn: From 19 to 30 March 2012 a group of Polish researchers from the Wroclaw University of Economics are exchanging experiences with IUCE researchers — more specifically with the GRIAL Group — on models of university training and eLearning, and will take part in various events organised at the University with the aim of promoting…
contentEn:
From 19 to 30 March 2012 a group of Polish researchers from the Wroclaw University of Economics are exchanging experiences with IUCE researchers — more specifically with the GRIAL Group — on models of university training and eLearning, and will take part in various events organised at the University with the aim of promoting mobility between the partner institutions.

The GRIAL Group takes part as a partner in the European project TALARIA (Teaching and E-­Learning Advances in European Mobility Space), coordinated by the Wroclaw University of Economics (Poland) within the Leonardo Da Vinci Mobility VETPRO programme. The project lasts one year and is divided into two stages. In the first stage, currently in full swing, the main objective is the exchange of experiences in the use of ICT methods and tools to support teaching in the different education systems of the partners involved in the project.

titleEn: Teaching innovation technologies
excerptEn: The IUCE took part, on 13 March in Madrid, in the presentation of an innovative virtual environment for medical training, based on the use of teaching innovation technologies. The Deputy Director of the Institute and Professor of Anatomy, Juan A. Juanes, took part in the event with the Centre for Imaging and…
contentEn:
The IUCE took part, on 13 March in Madrid, in the presentation of an innovative virtual environment for medical training, based on the use of teaching innovation technologies. The Deputy Director of the Institute and Professor of Anatomy, Juan A. Juanes, took part in the event with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology (CITEC-B), led by Juan José Gómez. The event was also attended by the director of the Innovation Unit of the Hospital Clínico San Carlos in Madrid, Dr Julio Mayo.

The University Institute for Research in Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, through the Framework Collaboration Agreement between the University of Salamanca and the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology (CITEC-B) of the Community of Madrid, presents its own gateway for teaching innovation at the service of medical and health training, through the HERMES platform.

titleEn: Evaluation of University Teaching Quality
excerptEn: The IUCE is taking part in another Studies and Analysis Project of the Ministry of Education on the Evaluation of University Teaching Quality. The project EA2011-0113: Analysis of Teaching Quality Evaluation Tools is led by the University of Valladolid, with the participation of the four public universities of the region, together with the University of Deusto, the University…
contentEn:
The IUCE is taking part in another Studies and Analysis Project of the Ministry of Education on the Evaluation of University Teaching Quality.

The project EA2011-0113: Analysis of Teaching Quality Evaluation Tools is led by the University of Valladolid, with the participation of the four public universities of the region, together with the University of Deusto, the University of Valencia, the University of Castilla-La Mancha and two foreign universities, from Chile and Denmark.

titleEn: Presentation of the Hermes Platform
excerptEn: On 23 February 2012 the Hermes Platform for teaching innovation at the service of health was presented at the Rectorate of the University of Salamanca, joining the efforts of teaching and medical teams in fostering and disseminating scientific research activities in the educational sphere, under an agreement with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge…
contentEn:
On 23 February 2012 the Hermes Platform for teaching innovation at the service of health was presented at the Rectorate of the University of Salamanca, joining the efforts of teaching and medical teams in fostering and disseminating scientific research activities in the educational sphere, under an agreement with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology (CITEC-B) of the Community of Madrid. The IUCE promotes the use of technological methods for training and knowledge visualisation, and the exploration of their teaching potential.

The event was attended by:

  • José Ángel Domínguez, Vice-Rector for Teaching.
  • Mª José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the IUCE.
  • Juan Antonio Juanes, Deputy Director of the IUCE.
  • Félix Ortega, Academic Secretary of the IUCE.
  • Leonardo Sánchez De Lerin, Business Development Director of CITEC-B.

titleEn: Distinction for a professional career
excerptEn: The IUCE would like to congratulate our colleague Yolanda Arias, who on 22 February received, from the hands of Rector Daniel Hernández Ruipérez, the distinction awarded to Administration and Services Staff for her long and outstanding professional career, spanning 35 years at the University of Salamanca and, more specifically, at the IUCE. Thank you very much, Yolanda!
contentEn:
The IUCE would like to congratulate our colleague Yolanda Arias, who on 22 February received, from the hands of Rector Daniel Hernández Ruipérez, the distinction awarded to Administration and Services Staff for her long and outstanding professional career, spanning 35 years at the University of Salamanca and, more specifically, at the IUCE.

Thank you very much, Yolanda!

titleEn: In memoriam
excerptEn: The worlds of education and culture are in mourning following the news of the death of Germán Sánchez Ruipérez in the early hours of Sunday 12 February 2012, while he was spending a few days' rest in the Dominican Republic with his wife Ofelia. Among the many honours he received throughout…
contentEn:
The worlds of education and culture are in mourning following the news of the death of Germán Sánchez Ruipérez in the early hours of Sunday 12 February 2012, while he was spending a few days' rest in the Dominican Republic with his wife Ofelia.

Among the many honours he received throughout his life, the Honorary Doctorate in Education Sciences from the University of Salamanca was for him one of the most cherished; as he himself remarked, “today I am living in a dream from which I would never wish to wake”.

This Institute, as the promoter of that event just one year ago, mourns his loss and believes that the best tribute we can offer him is to continue working and collaborating with the legacy he has left us: his most cherished work, the Foundation that bears his name.

The IUCE will be with his family and friends at the funeral, which is expected to be held at the Church of La Purísima on Thursday 16 February at 1 pm.

Rest in peace, Dr Germán Sánchez Ruipérez.

titleEn: Teaching innovation at the service of health
excerptEn: The IUCE joins the HERMES platform, opening its own gateway for teaching innovation at the service of medical and health training. The Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology (CITEC-B) in Madrid collaborates with the IUCE through the HermesCloud Platform, with the aim of fostering…
contentEn:
The IUCE joins the HERMES platform, opening its own gateway for teaching innovation at the service of medical and health training.

The Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology (CITEC-B) in Madrid collaborates with the IUCE through the HermesCloud Platform, with the aim of fostering and putting into practice scientific research initiatives in Education, technological development and transfer to the educational sphere.

titleEn: 2012 R&D&I Projects
excerptEn: In BOE no. 315, dated 31 December 2011, p. 147506 ff., the Resolution of 30 December 2011 of the Secretariat of State for Research, Development and Innovation is published, approving the 2012 call under the procedure for awarding grants corresponding to the subprogramme…
contentEn:
In BOE no. 315, dated 31 December 2011, p. 147506 ff., the Resolution of 30 December 2011 of the Secretariat of State for Research, Development and Innovation is published, approving the 2012 call under the procedure for awarding grants corresponding to the subprogramme of non-oriented fundamental research projects, within the framework of the 6th National Plan for Scientific Research, Development and Technological Innovation 2008-2011.

Website for applying for R&D&I projects.

titleEn: Project for development in Latin America through higher education
excerptEn: The EVALfor Research Group of the University of Cádiz, with which several members of the IUCE collaborate, coordinates the project “Development of professional competences through participatory assessment and simulation using web tools (DevalSimWeb)”, with a cost of 1.2 million euros, 80% of which is funded by the European institutions.…
contentEn:
The EVALfor Research Group of the University of Cádiz, with which several members of the IUCE collaborate, coordinates the project “Development of professional competences through participatory assessment and simulation using web tools (DevalSimWeb)”, with a cost of 1.2 million euros, 80% of which is funded by the European institutions.

Besides the University of Cádiz, the project involves the University of Strathclyde (United Kingdom), the University of Antioquia (Colombia), the Pontifical Catholic University of Ecuador-Ibarra Campus, the University of Costa Rica and the Salesian University of Bolivia. The purpose of the ALFA III programme of the European Commission, within which the project is framed, is to promote higher education in Latin America as a means of contributing to the economic and social development of the region.

The project aims to improve the quality of higher education by developing the assessment competence of teaching staff and students, together with the interpersonal competences involved (critical thinking, creativity/innovation, teamwork, autonomy, etc.), as a means of enabling students to complete their university studies and enter the labour market in a context characterised by the use of information and communication technologies through social and knowledge networks.

titleEn: 2012 PDI Training
excerptEn: At the initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching, the IUCE has drawn up the 2012 Teaching Training Plan of the University of Salamanca (Governing Council, 30-11-2011). The information booklet sets out the activities to be carried out and all the details relating to each of them, both for the General Training Programme, drawn up by the…
contentEn:
At the initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching, the IUCE has drawn up the 2012 Teaching Training Plan of the University of Salamanca (Governing Council, 30-11-2011).

The information booklet sets out the activities to be carried out and all the details relating to each of them, both for the General Training Programme, drawn up by the IUCE, and for the In-Centre Training Programme, prepared at the request of the centres themselves.

The pre-enrolment period will run from 9 to 16 January 2012.

General Training Programme.

In-Centre Training Programme.
